D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Teal & Orange — Complementary Cinema Look

push the highlights toward orange (skin tones) and the shadows toward teal — opposite hues sit across the colour wheel and visually separate subject from background

The teal-orange look has dominated commercial cinema since the 2000s. The recipe is simple: shadows and shadows-only acquire a cyan/teal cast (~190°), highlights and skin tones a warm orange (~30°). Because 190° and 30° are nearly complementary on the colour wheel, the contrast is perceptually maximised — and skin happens to fall on the warm side.

The before/after panels show a frame-graph thumbnail re-rendered. Slide the strength up — at 0 the grade is neutral; at 100 it is extreme. The chromaticity plot on the right shows how the patch population moves toward the teal-orange axis.
